text.title: Solo Saas Legal Docs Pack Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Solo Saas Legal Docs Pack. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Pack contains EXACTLY 5 docs: ToS, Privacy, Cookie, Refund, DPA.
rule.rationale: Pack contains EXACTLY 5 docs: ToS, Privacy, Cookie, Refund, DPA.

rule.statement: Each doc sourced from a named template (Stripe / Iubenda / Termly / Vanta starter); no greenfield prose.
rule.rationale: Each doc sourced from a named template (Stripe / Iubenda / Termly / Vanta starter); no greenfield prose.

rule.statement: Billing activation is BLOCKED until all 5 docs are live at stable URLs.
rule.rationale: Billing activation is BLOCKED until all 5 docs are live at stable URLs.

rule.statement: Each doc names a named owner + review date (12-month decay).
rule.rationale: Each doc names a named owner + review date (12-month decay).

rule.statement: Standard is 'good enough to bill', not 'lawyer-reviewed'; schedule lawyer review for later.
rule.rationale: Standard is 'good enough to bill', not 'lawyer-reviewed'; schedule lawyer review for later.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-saas-legal-docs-pack.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
